content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN positive integers.

Suppose F(B,X)F(B,X)F(B,X) gives the **minimum** positive integer YYY such that:


XXX divides Y⋅BiY \cdot B_iY⋅Bi​ for all (1≤i≤∣B∣)(1 \leq i \leq |B|)(1≤i≤∣B∣).
Find the value of F(A,Ai)F(A,A_i)F(A,Ai​) for all (1≤i≤N)(1 \le i \le N)(1≤i≤N).


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the length of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ representing the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, print NNN space-separated integers where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes the value F(A,Ai)F(A,A_i)F(A,Ai​).
constraints: 1 ≤ N ≤ 5·10^5 (sum of N over all test cases ≤ 5·10^5)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated positive integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with N space‑separated integers. The i‑th integer is F(A, Ai), i.e., the minimum positive Y such that Ai divides Y·Aj for every j (1 ≤ j ≤ N).

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: gcd(1,2)=1, answers are 1/1=1 and 2/1=2. Test case 2: gcd(2,2,2)=2, each answer is 2/2=1.
gcd of all numbers is 62500. Dividing each element by 62500 yields the required values.
The gcd of the array is 1, so each answer equals the original element.

solution.approach: For a fixed i we need the smallest Y such that Ai | Y·Aj for all j. For each j the condition is equivalent to Ai / gcd(Ai, Aj) dividing Y. Therefore Y must be a multiple of the least common multiple of all values (Ai / gcd(Ai, Aj)). Using prime factorisation it can be shown that this LCM equals Ai divided by the gcd of the whole array. Hence the answer for every position i is simply Ai / G, where G = gcd(A1, A2, …, AN). The algorithm: compute G by iterating over the array, then output Ai / G for each i. This runs in O(N) per test case and O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (overall O(total N)).

code.Python3:
import sys, math

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    t = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(n)]
        g = 0
        for v in arr:
            g = v if g == 0 else math.gcd(g, v)
        out_lines.append(' '.join(str(v // g) for v in arr))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
